title: erigeron compositus subsp. discoideus
intro: Erigeron compositus subsp. discoideus, also known as the mountain fleabane or mountain daisy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Erigeron compositus subsp. discoideus are believed to provide various health benefits, including wound healing, pain relief, and antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat minor injuries, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
